title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: Read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

content.text: The Great Library of Alexandria remains one of the most (0) iconic symbols of knowledge in human history. Established in the third century BCE, it (1) to house the entire sum of human wisdom within its walls. Scholars from across the Mediterranean were (2) to the city, making it a vibrant center for scientific and literary innovation. The library’s collection was vast, reportedly containing hundreds of thousands of papyrus scrolls that (3) a wide range of subjects, from mathematics to poetry. To (4) the collection, officials would often search arriving ships for books, which were then copied and returned to their owners. However, the fate of the library is (5) in mystery, with various theories suggesting it was destroyed by fire during several different conflicts. This loss of ancient knowledge is often (6) as a tragic turning point for Western civilization. Despite its physical disappearance, the legacy of the library (7) on in the modern concept of universal access to information. It (8) as a powerful reminder of the fragility of human achievement and the importance of preserving our cultural heritage for future generations, ensuring that the wisdom of the past is never truly forgotten by the people of the world.

example.explanation: “Iconic symbol” is a common collocation for something very famous and representative of an era.

questions.explanation: “Aimed to [verb]” is used to describe a specific goal or purpose.
“Drawn to” is a common passive structure meaning to be attracted to a place or idea.
“Covered subjects” is the standard way to describe the scope of a collection or study.
“Expand a collection” is the correct collocation for increasing the size of a library or archive.
“Shrouded in mystery” is a fixed idiomatic expression.
“Cited as” means to be mentioned as an example or reason for something.
“Lives on” is a phrasal verb meaning to continue to exist after something has gone.
“Serves as a reminder” is a fixed formal collocation.
